Safety Mar 27, 2026

La Guardia Crash: NTSB Releases Footage & Additional Info Regarding Tragedy

You are probably well-aware by now that an Air Canada Express Bombardier CRJ900 crashed into a fire truck at La Guardia airport on the night of March 22nd, 2026. While, on the surface, the tragic accident that took the lives of the two aircraft pilots looks to be a straightforward case of...

NTSB investigation findings into the La Guardia CRJ900 crash will likely reshape airport ground operations safety protocols and fire truck positioning procedures at major hubs.

See Simple Flying →

Aerospace Mar 27, 2026

Industry says proposed NASA changes to commercial space station plans create confusion

NASA’s proposed changes to its support of commercial space stations have created concern and confusion among companies developing them, the head of an industry organization warned.

NASA's ambiguous transition timeline for commercial space station support risks delaying private sector investments critical to maintaining continuous U.S. orbital capabilities post-ISS.

See SpaceNews →

London Heathrow's Big Shake-Up: The 3 New Long-Haul Airlines You'll See In 2026

They add to the three long-haul airlines that started flying to Heathrow last year...

Heathrow's expansion of long-haul carriers signals recovery in transatlantic and Asian route competition, reflecting pent-up demand post-pandemic and potential slot availability improvements.

Commercial Mar 27, 2026

Breakthrough: United Airlines flight attendants set to vote on new contract

United Airlines flight attendants are set to vote on a new tentative agreement being proposed by the US…...

United's flight attendant contract ratification will signal labor market dynamics across major carriers and influence upcoming negotiations at competitors.

Safety Mar 27, 2026

Selecting Risk Controls and Accepting Residual Risk

Where Safety Risk Management moves from analysis to action — how organizations develop controls under §5.55(c)–(d).

Effective SRM requires organizations to systematically justify why residual risks remain acceptable after implementing feasible controls, moving beyond theoretical analysis to documented risk acceptance decisions.

Discover the top 5 US airlines that are redefining luxury in the skies with their state-of-the-art business class seats.

Premium seat investments are becoming competitive differentiators as US carriers battle for high-yield business travelers in an increasingly commoditized transatlantic market.

Commercial Mar 27, 2026

Star Alliance opens new Connection Centre at LAX to speed up tight transfers

Star Alliance has opened a new Star Connection Center at Los Angeles International Airport (LAX), its ninth such…...

Star Alliance's ninth dedicated connection center at LAX addresses a critical operational bottleneck by physically segregating tight-transfer passengers, reducing missed connections and improving hub efficiency.

New NTSB footage shows staggering scale of LaGuardia crash tragedy

Visual evidence from crash sites dramatically amplifies public understanding of accident severity, potentially influencing safety investment priorities across the industry.

FAA investigates United Airlines 737 and Black Hawk close call in Santa Ana

A California National Guard UH-60M Black Hawk helicopter crossed in front of a United Airlines Boeing 737-800 on…...

Airspace incursions involving military rotorcraft near commercial airports highlight coordination gaps between civilian and military aviation operations in controlled airspace.

Safety Mar 27, 2026

The Evolution of Aviation SMS

From the Wright brothers to the 2024 FAA rule — how aviation safety evolved from reactive investigation to proactive risk management.

Modern SMS transforms aviation safety from incident-driven responses to systematic risk prediction, reducing accidents before they occur rather than learning from them.

Military Mar 27, 2026

US diverts Swiss F-35 funds to cover frozen Patriot payments

The United States has circumvented a Swiss payment freeze on the Patriot air defense system by redirecting funds…...

US budget reallocation tactics for frozen defense payments reveal how geopolitical constraints force creative financing solutions in military procurement.

Commercial Mar 27, 2026

Boeing Boost: Major African Carrier Doubles Down On 737 MAX With 10 New Jets

The new aircraft will come quicker than you might expect.

Accelerated delivery timelines for the 737 MAX suggest Boeing's production capacity is improving faster than industry observers anticipated, signaling reduced supply chain constraints.

See Simple Flying →

Aerospace Mar 27, 2026

SBQuantum and Spire to send quantum diamond magnetometer into orbit

SAN FRANCISCO – Canadian startup SBQuantum plans to send a quantum diamond magnetometer into low-Earth orbit March 30 on a Spire Global satellite flying on a SpaceX Falcon 9 rideshare. Spire is providing the satellite, ground stations and data processing for SBQuantum’s magnetometer, which is...

Quantum sensing technology is advancing toward operational space deployment, potentially enabling new Earth observation and atmospheric monitoring capabilities for commercial satellite operators.

See SpaceNews →

Aerospace Mar 27, 2026

ispace redesigns lunar lander, introduces lunar communications service

Japanese company ispace is revising its lunar lander design and further delaying the first mission by its American subsidiary while also unveiling plans for a lunar satellite constellation.

ispace's pivot toward lunar communications infrastructure signals a shift from one-off landing missions to establishing sustained orbital services, positioning the company as a potential space utility provider.

See SpaceNews →

▶
Safety Mar 27, 2026

RNO Mustang 9 Departure

Pilots departing from runways 17 left and right at Reno-Tahoe International need to pay special attention to the Mustang 9 Departure. It is extremely important that the procedure on the Mustang 9...

The Mustang 9 departure procedure at RNO requires strict adherence due to terrain and airspace constraints that create safety-critical compliance points for departing flights.

See Federal Aviation Administration (YouTube) →

Commercial Mar 27, 2026

Qantas Scraps Nonstop Perth-Paris Route In Major European Network Shake-Up

Qantas shakes up its international network to offer more flights to Europe via Singapore.

Qantas is pivoting from direct ultra-long-haul routes to a hub-and-spoke model through Singapore, prioritizing frequency and connectivity over nonstop service to maximize passenger throughput.

See Simple Flying →

Military Mar 27, 2026

Lockheed Martin F-35 workers open contract talks in Fort Worth

The union representing around 5,000 workers building the F-35 at Lockheed Martin’s Fort Worth, Texas, plant has opened…...

Labor negotiations at Lockheed's primary F-35 production facility could impact delivery timelines and costs for a program critical to allied air forces worldwide.

See AeroTime Hub →

Commercial Mar 27, 2026

The Striking Differences Between The MD-11 & DC-10

Though closely related, the MD-11 and DC-10 differed in range, cockpit design, aerodynamics, and ultimately commercial success.

The MD-11's advanced aerodynamics and digital cockpit failed to overcome structural similarities that limited cargo appeal versus the DC-10's proven freighter dominance.

See Simple Flying →

Drones Mar 27, 2026

TEKEVER successfully integrates Quadsat Electronic Warfare Payload SpectraLoc onto AR3 EVO Drone Platform

London, England — TEKEVER, a leading provider of advanced AI-based autonomous systems, and Quadsat have successfully...

Integration of sophisticated EW payloads onto medium-altitude drones enables operators to conduct electronic warfare missions with extended endurance and reduced platform costs compared to manned aircraft.

Safety Mar 27, 2026

Surface Safety Spotlight – Los Angeles International Airport, CA (LAX) – From the Flight Deck

Los Angeles International Airport (LAX) is one of the nation’s busiest commercial airports. This short video highlights a runway incursion hazard where pilots exiting Runway 24R, particularly via...

Runway exit procedures at high-traffic airports like LAX pose recurring incursion risks that require pilots to maintain heightened vigilance during ground operations.

Commercial Mar 27, 2026

The US Domestic Routes Operated By Widebody Aircraft

Some of the country's busiest domestic routes are operated by widebodies.

Widebody deployment on domestic routes reflects carrier capacity strategies to maximize revenue on high-demand corridors despite higher operating costs per seat.

Safety Mar 27, 2026

United Airlines Jet & Military Helicopter Separated By Only 500 Feet Over Orange County

A close call in the sunny skies of SoCal.

Near-miss incidents in Class B airspace highlight gaps in real-time coordination between civilian ATC and military operations, raising questions about current separation standard adequacy.

Aerospace Mar 27, 2026

Satellite Spots a Spawn

The activity of herring around Vancouver Island in British Columbia brightened coastal waters enough to be detectable from space.

Multispectral satellite technology originally developed for aviation and Earth observation now enables marine biology monitoring, demonstrating aerospace assets' expanding civilian applications.

See NASA →

Commercial Mar 27, 2026

Here’s How Much Range The Airbus A350-1000 Has Compared To The Boeing 777-300ER

The Airbus A350-1000 and Boeing 777-300ER are long-haul aviation titans, but the A350's slight edge in range may not be as decisive as it seems.

The A350-1000's marginal range advantage becomes negligible when operators factor in payload-range tradeoffs and real-world fuel planning, making aircraft selection dependent on route economics rather than specifications alone.

See Simple Flying →

Industry Mar 27, 2026

Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization

Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.

Operational optimization through technology integration offers the most immediate pathway to accommodate projected traffic growth while reducing aviation's environmental footprint before new aircraft designs reach widespread adoption.

See MDPI →

eVTOL Mar 27, 2026

AIR claims $1 billion in orders as eVTOL maker pushes personal aircraft

Israel-based electric aircraft developer AIR has declared that it has surpassed $1 billion in orders, driven by demand…...

AIR's $1 billion order book signals that early eVTOL demand extends beyond air taxi operators to include personal aircraft buyers seeking alternative mobility.

See AeroTime Hub →

Commercial Mar 27, 2026

Here’s How Much Larger The GE9X Is Compared To The GE90

The engine is set to enter commercial service on the upcoming Boeing 777X.

The GE9X's significantly larger dimensions enable higher bypass ratios that improve fuel efficiency by up to 10% compared to earlier generations, directly impacting 777X operating economics.

See Simple Flying →

Aerospace Mar 26, 2026

U.S. GSSAP satellites execute GEO handoff to monitor China’s Shijian-29 spacecraft

Commercial space tracking data shows U.S. satellites coordinating maneuvers to maintain proximity and continuous observation of a pair of Chinese spacecraft in geostationary orbit.

Real-time orbital maneuvering coordination demonstrates how U.S. space surveillance assets maintain persistent intelligence on foreign spacecraft activities in contested GEO regions.

See SpaceNews →

Military Mar 26, 2026

Space Command begins phased move to Alabama

The new headquarters building is expected to be completed by 2031...

The relocation consolidates U.S. military space operations into a single facility, potentially reshaping defense contractor presence and aerospace supplier networks across the Southeast through 2031.

See SpaceNews →

Aerospace Mar 26, 2026

What’s Up: April 2026 Skywatching Tips from NASA

Mercury shines at its brightest for the year, the Lyrid meteor shower peaks, and a bright new comet makes an appearance in April’s night sky.

April's celestial events provide optimal visibility windows for pilot training and navigation planning using natural reference points.

See NASA →

Aerospace Mar 26, 2026

La NASA presentará el telescopio Roman completo y ofrecerá una conferencia de prensa

Se invita a los medios de comunicación el martes 21 de abril al Centro de Vuelo Espacial Goddard de la NASA en Greenbelt, Maryland, para conocer el telescopio espacial Nancy Grace Roman de la agencia, cuya construcción terminó recientemente y el cual se encuentra ultimando las pruebas previas a su...

NASA's Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope completes construction and enters final pre-launch testing, positioning the agency for expanded deep-space imaging capabilities beyond current observatories.

See NASA →

Aerospace Mar 26, 2026

Government use of commercial procurement models has limitations in space

While government agencies are increasingly turning to commercial contracting mechanisms for space capabilities, that does not necessarily translate to additional business opportunities for the companies offering them.

Government space procurement shifts toward commercial models, yet agencies' strict requirements and limited volume may not expand actual contract opportunities despite modernized purchasing frameworks.

See SpaceNews →

Aerospace Mar 26, 2026

NASA to Unveil Complete Roman Telescope, Host Media Briefing

Media are invited Tuesday, April 21, to NASA’s Goddard Space Flight Center in Greenbelt, Maryland, for a look at the agency’s Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope, which recently completed construction and is wrapping up prelaunch testing. This will be one of the last opportunities to view the fully...

Roman's completion marks a critical milestone toward launching a space observatory capable of imaging exoplanets and dark energy, with immediate relevance to aerospace industrial suppliers and launch providers.

See NASA →

Aerospace Mar 26, 2026

NASA Releases Artemis II Moon Mission Launch Countdown

Before NASA sends its astronauts Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, Christina Koch, and CSA (Canadian Space Agency) astronaut Jeremy Hansen on their Artemis II mission around the Moon, the launch team at NASA’s Kennedy Space Center in Florida and teams across the country will begin counting down about...

Artemis II's international crew composition signals NASA's strategy to strengthen partnerships while advancing lunar exploration capabilities beyond the Apollo era.

See NASA →

Navi AI launches publicly with $6 million to expand AI pilot training platform

Navi AI has launched publicly with more than $6 million in funding, as it looks to expand its…...

AI-powered pilot training platforms are attracting significant venture capital, signaling industry recognition that synthetic training could reduce costs and accelerate pilot pipeline development.

Aerospace Mar 26, 2026

Station Nation: Aaron Rose, Cold Stowage Mission Manager

As a member of the Crew and Thermal Systems Division, Aaron Rose supports critical cargo resupply missions to the International Space Station. In this role, he works with payload developers to safely transport temperature-controlled science experiments to and from station with portable coolers,...

Cold chain logistics expertise is becoming a specialized aerospace mission-critical function as ISS research increasingly requires precise thermal management for scientific payloads.
